plugin.name: Multi-language
plugin.description: WPML-like multilingual content support for GoPress: language-aware content records, menu assignment, UI string translations, and theme setting translations.
plugin.settings.title: %s - Plugin Settings
plugin.multilang.ui.002: Manage the languages supported by the site. The default language has no URL prefix; other languages are prefixed automatically, for example /en/products/....
plugin.multilang.ui.021: Review translation status for all content. Click + to clone content into a target language, or edit an existing translation.
plugin.multilang.ui.029: No content yet. Create content in
plugin.multilang.ui.030: first, then return here to manage translations.
plugin.multilang.ui.032: Assign menus for each language at every menu location. The system displays the matching menu when the language changes.
plugin.multilang.ui.036: Menu locations are registered by the active theme, such as header or footer.
plugin.multilang.ui.038: create a separate menu for each language.
plugin.multilang.ui.039: Return here, choose the correct menu for each language and location, then click Save Menu Assignment.
plugin.multilang.ui.040: When the language changes, the matching menu is shown and local links receive the language prefix.
plugin.multilang.ui.041: The active theme has not registered menu locations. Check whether the theme calls
plugin.multilang.ui.043: Manage UI string translations from the active theme. Defaults come from theme locale files; overrides are stored in the database. Clear an input to use the file default.
plugin.multilang.ui.049: The active theme does not provide locale files. Add JSON files under the theme
plugin.multilang.ui.052: Manage translatable theme setting copy such as titles, descriptions, and button text. Default-language values come from the theme settings page; enter translations for other languages here.
plugin.multilang.ui.056: The active theme has not registered translatable setting fields. The theme must call
plugin.multilang.ui.059: The current mode uses language prefixes. The default language has no prefix; other languages use URLs like /en/path...
plugin.multilang.ui.067: Each content item can have multiple language versions linked by a translation group (trid). Each version is an independent Content record with its own title, body, and slug.
plugin.multilang.ui.068: Content edit pages will show a translation status panel soon.
plugin.multilang.ui.069: Click Add Translation to clone the current content into the target language.
plugin.multilang.ui.070: Translated content has its own URL:
plugin.multilang.ui.078: Translate UI strings provided by core i18n.
plugin.multilang.ui.079: Get the current request language code from core i18n.
plugin.multilang.ui.080: Generate a URL with language prefix from core i18n.
plugin.multilang.ui.081: Theme navigation end slot; this plugin renders the language switcher there when enabled.
plugin.multilang.ui.083: The plugin uses four separate tables:
plugin.multilang.ui.111: Manage translations for the core site name and site description. Front-end SEO titles, meta descriptions, and theme templates use these values for the current language.
plugin.multilang.ui.113: There are no translatable site setting fields.
plugin.multilang.js.language_required: Please enter a language code and name
plugin.multilang.js.clone_to: The current content will be cloned to %s (%s).
